sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click all green blocks except (31, 49) which is above player
# Safe blocks to click:
safe_clicks = [
    (3, 32),    # (1, 31)
    (9, 32),    # (7, 31)
    (33, 14),    # (31, 13)
    (33, 38),    # (31, 37)
    (33, 44),    # (31, 43)
    # (33, 50) - SKIP (31, 49) - above player
    (39, 14),    # (37, 13)
    (39, 20),    # (37, 19)
    (39, 26),    # (37, 25)
    (39, 32),    # (37, 31)
    (45, 14),    # (43, 13)
    (45, 20),    # (43, 19)
    (45, 26),    # (43, 25)
    (45, 32),    # (43, 31)
]

for r, c in safe_clicks:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    state = last_action_result.get('state', 'N/A')
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 10]
    print(f"Click ({r}, {c}): state={state}, greens={len(greens)}")
    if state in ('GAME_OVER', 'LEVEL_COMPLETED', 'DONE'):
        break

# Now move player away from col 50 and click the last block
if last_action_result.get('state', 'N/A') not in ('GAME_OVER', 'LEVEL_COMPLETED', 'DONE'):
    # Move player left to be under a safe column
    for _ in range(5):
        action([{'action': 'LEFT'}])
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 6:
            b = n['boundary']
            player_c = min(c for r,c in b)
            print(f"\nPlayer moved to col {player_c}")
            break
    
    # Click the last block at (31, 49)
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 50}])
    state = last_action_result.get('state', 'N/A')
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 10]
    print(f"Click last block: state={state}, greens={len(greens)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
